semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:sec-proj-formula}
  Let $s,t\in\operatorname{NS}(R)$ be sections of heights $m$ and $n$ respectively. Then
  \[
    \Pi(s)\cdot\Pi(t) =
    s\cdot t - 1 - (m+n).
  \]
  In particular, $\Pi(s)^2=-2-2m$.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lem}\label{lem:g-g-bisections-biject} Let $\mathcal{B}_{g,n}\subset\mathcal{B}_g$ be the set of divisor classes of arithmetic genus $g$, height $n$ bisections. Then the restriction ${\ensuremath{\left.\Pi\right|_{\mathcal{B}_{g,g}}}}:\mathcal{B}_{g,g}\to\operatorname{ZF}^\perp(R)$ is a bijection onto the set of $u\in\operatorname{ZF}^\perp(R)$ of norm $-2(g+2)$. In particular, by Proposition~\ref{prop:e8-and-e4}, $\#\mathcal{B}_{g,g}$ is the coefficient of $q^{g+2}$ in $E_4(q)$. \end{lem}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop}\label{prop:jacobianideal} If $s_1,\dots, s_n\in B$ are isolated singularities with local analytic equations $y^2=x^{d_i}$, then the first order equisingular deformations of $B$ in its linear system are given by $$\mathrm{H}^0(B,L_B\otimes \mathrm{Jac}(B))\subset \mathrm{H}^0(B,L_B),$$ where $\mathrm{Jac}(B)$ is the Jacobian ideal cosupported on $\{s_1,\dots,s_n\}$ defined locally by $(y,x^{d_i-1})$. \end{prop}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
Let $\pi:R \to \mathbb P^1$ be a rational elliptic surface with irreducible fibers, and $L$ a bisection line bundle. Then the following conditions are equivalent.
    \begin{enumerate}[(i)]
        \item $L$ is Weierstrass.
        \item The complete linear system $\vert{}L\rvert{}$ contains a divisor of the form $2s+mf$, for $s$ a section of $\pi$ and $f$ a fiber of $\pi$.
        \item The projection $\Pi(L)\in\operatorname{ZF}^\perp(R)\cong\E8(-1)$ is 2-divisible, i.e., $\Pi(L)=2u$ for some $u\in\operatorname{ZF}^\perp(R)$ for any choice of zero section as in \S\ref{subsec:elliptic-surfaces}.
    \end{enumerate}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:res-covers-hirzebruch}
The Hirzebruch surface $\mathbb P(\pi_*(L))$ is either $\mathbb F_0$, $\mathbb F_1$, or $\mathbb F_2$. The first two cases occur for $L$ ordinary (and $g$ even, odd, respectively), and the third case occurs for $L$ Weierstrass ($g$ is always even in that case).

\label{lem:vanishingtheorem}
    Let $L$ be a bisection line bundle on $R$, and let $B\in\vert{}L\rvert{}$ be a rational bisection. Consider the ideal sheaf $\mathcal{I}\subset\mathcal O_B$ cosupported on the singularities $\{s_1,\dots,s_n\}$ of $B$ defined locally as follows. If $s_i$ is a node of $B$, then $\mathcal I_{s_i} = (y,x)$. If $s_i$ is a singularity of type $y^2 = x^{d_i}$ with $d_i\geq 3$, then $\mathcal I_{s_i} = (y,x^{\delta(d_i)+1})$. Then $\mathrm{H}^1(B,L_B\otimes \mathcal I)\cong 0$.

We prove that $B$ has only nodal singularities, and moreover that it has a 1-dimensional space of first order equisingular deformations. A key observation is that the local defining equation of a bisection near any singular point must be $y^2 = x^d$, with $d\geq 2$, so it suffices to rule out $A_{d-1}$ singularities for $d\geq 3$ on a general $B\in V(L)$.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{thm:equisingular}
Let $B\in |L|$ be an irreducible rational bisection curve. Then $B$ has at most one singularity that is worse than nodal. If $B$ has such a singularity, then the space of first order equisingular deformations of $B$ is trivial, and otherwise it is 1-dimensional.

\label{prop:galois}
Let $\Sigma \subset X$ be a section of $\pi'$. Then either (i) $\Sigma$ is the base change of a section of $\pi$, or (ii) $\Sigma$ is a component of $(u')^{-1}(B)$, where $B$ is a bisection of $\pi$ with the same branch points as $u$.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{lem:min-height-realizable}
  For any bisection line bundle $L=\mathcal O(B)$ of genus $g$, there exists a section $s$ such that $s\cdot B=g-\delta_g$, where $\delta_g\in\set{0,1}$ only depends on $g$. In fact, $\delta_g=1$ if and only if $g \equiv 0 \mod 4$, and $L$ is Weierstrass.

lean_statement:
theorem prop_conjectural_degree_formula
  {R L : Type*} [AddCommGroup L] [Module ℝ L]
  (f_L : L → ℝ)
  (V_L : Set L)
  (DE_4_infty E_4_infty E_2_q2 DE_4_q4 E_4_q4 theta_q2 : ℝ)
  (g_L : ℕ)
  (Conjecture_multconj : Prop)
  (L_ordinary_or_Weierstrass : Prop)
  (h_conj : Conjecture_multconj)
  (h_L : L_ordinary_or_Weierstrass) :
  2 * (f_L '' V_L).ncard = (DE_4_infty - E_4_infty * E_2_q2) ∨
  2 * (f_L '' V_L).ncard = (1/2) * (DE_4_q4 - E_4_q4 * E_2_q2 - 12 * (theta_q2 - 1))

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:conjectural-degree-formula}
  If Conjecture~\ref{conj:multconj} holds, then for a general pair $(R,L)$, $2\deg({f_L}_*[\widetilde V(L)])$ is the coefficient of $q^{g(L)+2}$ in the series
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:conjectural-degree-formula}
    DE_{4,\infty} - E_{4,\infty} E_2(q^2)
    \quad\text{ or }\quad
    \frac12\Big(
    DE_4(q^4)
    -E_4(q^4)E_2(q^2)
    -12\big(\theta(q^2)-1\big)
    \Big)
  \end{equation}
  % When you work it out, it is natural to stick an extra $-1$ at the end inside the second parentheses, but it doesn't affect the proposition, and it's nice later not to have it.
  according to whether $L$ is ordinary or Weierstrass.
semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: for a general pair (R, L) Assumptions: Conjecture holds Conclusion: 2 deg(f_L * [V(L)]) is the coefficient of q^{g(L)+2} in the series DE_{4,∞} - E_{4,∞} E_2(q^2) or (1/2)(DE_4(q^4) - E_4(q^4)E_2(q^2) - 12(θ(q^2) - 1)) depending on whether L is ordinary or Weierstrass

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:telltale-counting-series}
  Let $L=\mathcal O(B)$ be a bisection line bundle of genus $g$. Then the number of simple telltales in the complete linear system $\vert{}L\rvert{}$ is the coefficient of $q^{g+2}$ in
  \[
    E_{4,\infty}(q)
    \quad\text{ or }\quad
    \frac12 E_4(q^4)+\frac12,
  \]
  according to whether $L$ is ordinary or Weierstrass, and for any $m>0$, the number of non-simple telltales in $\vert{}L\rvert{}$ with fiber-multiplicity $m$ is the coefficient of $q^{g+2-2m}$ in
  \[
    12E_{4,\infty}(q)
    \quad\text{ or }\quad
    12\left(\frac12 E_4(q^4)+\frac12\right),
  \]
  according to whether $L$ is ordinary or Weierstrass.

Conclusion: L=\mathcal O(B)

assumptions: \label{prop:telltale-counting-series} Let $L=\mathcal O(B)$ be a bisection line bundle of genus $g$.

Then the number of simple telltales in the complete linear system $\vert{}L\rvert{}$ is the coefficient of $q^{g+2}$ in \[ E_{4,\infty}(q) \quad\text{ or }\quad \frac12 E_4(q^4)+\frac12, \] according to whether $L$ is ordinary or Weierstrass, and for any $m>0$, the number of non-simple telltales in $\vert{}L\rvert{}$ with fiber-multiplicity $m$ is the coefficient of $q^{g+2-2m}$ in \[ 12E_{4,\infty}(q) \quad\text{ or }\quad 12\left(\frac12 E_4(q^4)+\frac12\right), \] according to whether $L$ is ordinary or Weierstrass.
